goal: Generate a 4:3 destination card image showing a family of four silhouettes on Clearwater Beach Pier 60 at golden hour sunset — sky on fire with orange-amber-pink, Gulf of Mexico turquoise-gold below, no faces, universal LATAM family silhouette, the #1 most photogenic Florida beach moment.

creative_direction.core_idea: Pier 60 sunset — la familia mirando el mejor atardecer de Florida, solo posible en carro desde Orlando
creative_direction.visual_thesis: Clearwater es 1h45 desde MCO. Este sunset es el reward del road trip

composition.shot_type: silhouette portrait — family backs to camera, facing sunset
composition.layout: Family of 4 silhouettes centered on Pier 60 — two adults flanking two children; sky fills the upper 55%; Gulf water with sunset reflections fills the lower 30%; pier structure frames top

camera.angle: eye level or slightly low — camera behind the family, looking at sunset with them
camera.lens_style: 85mm portrait — natural perspective, no distortion, sky and water both in frame
camera.depth_of_field: silhouettes sharp and black, sunset sky and Gulf water richly detailed

overlay_compatibility.text_safe_zone: upper right sky — deep orange or pink gradient, no silhouette overlap
overlay_compatibility.logo_safe_zone: lower left — white sand or dark pier base

subject.type: family silhouettes — no faces, backs to camera
subject.description: Family of four: two adults (parents) standing, two children between them — one smaller child possibly held up or pointing at the horizon. All silhouetted, no facial features visible.
critical_attributes: backs to camera — no faces at all
family of four — two adults, two children
silhouetted against the sunset — pure black outlines only
no beach towels or objects distracting from the silhouette
universal silhouette — no identifiable ethnicity markers

scene.location: Clearwater Beach, Pier 60, Pinellas County, FL — Gulf of Mexico facing west
scene.time_of_day: golden hour sunset — sun at or just below horizon, sky at maximum color intensity
environment: Pier 60 wooden structure visible — planks, railing silhouette framing top of image
Gulf of Mexico ahead and below — turquoise water now reflecting orange-gold sunset
white sand beach at the base of the pier in the foreground
sky is on fire: deep orange at horizon, amber mid-sky, pink-magenta upper sky
no commercial structures, no parasail boats — pure sky and water
scene.background_view: Gulf of Mexico sunset — horizon glowing orange, water reflecting amber-gold, pink sky above
critical_scene_rules: No faces visible — pure silhouettes only
Sky must be dramatic — orange-amber-pink gradient, not subtle or pastel
Gulf water must reflect the sunset — gold-orange glitter on turquoise base
Pier 60 structure must frame or be identifiable — it is the location anchor
White sand beach must be visible in the foreground

lighting.type: golden hour sunset — sun at or just below Gulf horizon
effects: sky is maximum color saturation: orange at horizon, amber above, pink-magenta at zenith
Gulf water surface breaks into orange-gold light reflection — dancing light on water
family silhouettes are pure black — no rim lighting, no fill — pure contre-jour
white sand in foreground picks up warm orange-pink glow
Pier 60 structure is a dark silhouette framing the top

rules: Brand amber #F0B576 appears naturally in the sky — no forced placement
Silhouettes must be pure black — no color bleeding into them
Sky color gradient must be dramatic and cinematic — not soft or pastel
Gulf water must show both turquoise base color and orange-gold sunset reflection

negative_prompt: no parasail or water sports equipment
no Atlantic coast — Gulf of Mexico only
no identifiable rental cars in frame
no crowded beach — pier only, minimal context
master_prompt: Create a 4:3 destination card image at 2000x1500 pixels for a car rental marketplace destination page showing the iconic Clearwater Beach Pier 60 golden hour sunset. Family of four silhouettes — two adults flanking two children, one child possibly pointing at the horizon — stands centered on Pier 60, all backs to camera, no faces visible, pure black silhouettes in contre-jour. Shot from behind the family at eye level with an 85mm lens, looking west toward the Gulf of Mexico sunset. The sky is dramatic and on fire: deep orange at the horizon, amber in the mid-sky, fading to pink-magenta above — maximum color saturation, not pastel. The Gulf of Mexico surface reflects the orange-gold sunset with dancing light on the turquoise-gold water. Pier 60's wooden structure and railing form a dark silhouette that frames the upper portion of the image. White sand beach is visible in the foreground, glowing warm orange-pink from the sunset. No commercial signage, no boats, no parasails, no faces, no text. The composition places the viewer behind the family, experiencing the sunset with them — the feeling of arrival, reward, and Florida perfection after a road trip from Orlando. The brand amber tone #F0B576 appears naturally in the mid-sky. Avoid faces, front-facing people, overcast sky, gray sunset, boats, commercial branding, Atlantic coast context, urban skyline, and any text or watermarks.
